alarms.name: alarms
alarms.shortDescription: Schedule code to run periodically or at a specific time.
alarms.detailedDescription: The Alarms API allows extensions to schedule code to run at a specific time in the future, or to run periodically. This is useful for background tasks like syncing data, checking for updates, or performing periodic maintenance. The alarms persist across browser restarts and are managed by the browser's alarm system.
mitigationTips: Use reasonable intervals to avoid excessive resource usage
Clear alarms when extension is disabled

bookmarks.name: bookmarks
bookmarks.shortDescription: Read, create, and modify your browser bookmarks.
bookmarks.detailedDescription: The Bookmarks API provides full access to the browser's bookmark system. Extensions can create, modify, reorganize, and delete bookmarks. This includes access to bookmark folders, the ability to search bookmarks, and monitor bookmark changes in real-time. All bookmark data, including titles, URLs, and folder structures, is accessible.
mitigationTips: Backup bookmarks before making bulk changes

browsingData.name: browsingData
browsingData.shortDescription: Clear browsing data (history, cache, cookies, etc.).
browsingData.detailedDescription: The BrowsingData API allows extensions to remove various types of browsing data from the user's profile. This includes history, downloads, cache, cookies, passwords, autofill data, and more. This is a destructive permission that can permanently erase user data without recovery options.
mitigationTips: Always confirm with user before clearing data
Provide granular options for what to clear
Show clear warnings about irreversible actions

contentSettings.name: contentSettings
contentSettings.shortDescription: Control website features (cookies, JavaScript, plugins, etc.) for specific sites.
contentSettings.detailedDescription: The ContentSettings API allows extensions to modify how the browser handles various content types on specific websites. This includes controlling JavaScript execution, cookie behavior, popups, notifications, camera/microphone access, and more. Changes can be applied globally or per-site.
mitigationTips: Provide clear UI for settings changes

contextMenus.name: contextMenus
contextMenus.shortDescription: Add items to the browser's right-click context menu.
contextMenus.detailedDescription: The ContextMenus API allows extensions to add custom items to the browser's context menu (right-click menu). These can appear in different contexts like page, selection, link, image, or editable areas. Clicking these items can trigger extension functionality.

cookies.name: cookies
cookies.shortDescription: Access, modify, and set browser cookies.
cookies.detailedDescription: The Cookies API provides programmatic access to the browser's cookie store. Extensions can read, modify, add, and delete cookies for any domain. This includes access to HTTP-only cookies, secure cookies, and session cookies. Combined with host permissions, this can access sensitive session data.
mitigationTips: Only access cookies for necessary domains
Secure cookie storage if caching locally

debugger.name: debugger
debugger.shortDescription: Access the browser's debugger protocol. Very powerful.
debugger.detailedDescription: The Debugger API allows extensions to attach to pages and use the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP). This provides extremely powerful capabilities including executing arbitrary JavaScript in page context, intercepting network requests, modifying DOM in real-time, and accessing all page data. This is essentially full control over web pages.
mitigationTips: Clearly inform users of the power this grants

declarativeContent.name: declarativeContent
declarativeContent.shortDescription: Show or hide UI elements based on page content without needing host permissions.
declarativeContent.detailedDescription: The DeclarativeContent API allows extensions to show or hide page actions based on the content of web pages without requiring broad host permissions. It uses declarative rules evaluated by the browser, making it more performant and privacy-friendly than programmatic content scripts.
mitigationTips: Use specific conditions to minimize performance impact
Combine with activeTab for content access when needed

declarativeNetRequest.name: declarativeNetRequest
declarativeNetRequest.shortDescription: Block or modify network requests using a static ruleset.
declarativeNetRequest.detailedDescription: The DeclarativeNetRequest API allows extensions to block or modify network requests using a declarative ruleset. Unlike webRequest, this is evaluated by the browser efficiently and doesn't require background script execution for every request. Rules are defined in JSON and loaded at install time.
mitigationTips: Keep rule sets reasonable in size
Test thoroughly to avoid breaking sites
Provide user controls for rule enablement

declarativeNetRequestWithHostAccess.name: declarativeNetRequestWithHostAccess
declarativeNetRequestWithHostAccess.shortDescription: Includes declarativeNetRequest features plus ability to redirect requests and modify headers.
declarativeNetRequestWithHostAccess.detailedDescription: This permission extends declarativeNetRequest to include dynamic capabilities like redirecting requests and modifying request/response headers. This requires host permissions for the affected URLs and provides more powerful network control than the base declarativeNetRequest.

desktopCapture.name: desktopCapture
desktopCapture.shortDescription: Capture screen, window, or tab content as a video stream.
desktopCapture.detailedDescription: The DesktopCapture API allows extensions to capture the contents of the screen, specific windows, or browser tabs as a media stream. This is used for screen recording, screen sharing, and screenshot tools. User consent is required via a picker dialog.
mitigationTips: Always show the native picker UI
Clearly indicate when recording is active

downloads.name: downloads
downloads.detailedDescription: The Downloads API allows extensions to programmatically initiate, monitor, control, and search downloads. Extensions can download files to disk, track progress, open downloaded files, and remove download history entries.
mitigationTips: Warn users before downloading multiple files

geolocation.name: geolocation
geolocation.shortDescription: Allow the extension to get your current geographical location.
geolocation.detailedDescription: The Geolocation permission allows extensions to access the device's geographical location using the standard Geolocation API. This requires user consent and provides latitude, longitude, and accuracy information.
mitigationTips: Provide option to manually enter location

history.name: history
history.shortDescription: Read and modify your browser history.
history.detailedDescription: The History API provides full read and write access to the browser's history database. Extensions can query visited URLs, add history entries, remove specific items or entire ranges, and monitor navigation events in real-time. This is highly sensitive user data.
mitigationTips: Never transmit history data externally without explicit consent
Allow users to disable history access

identity.name: identity
identity.shortDescription: Get OAuth2 access tokens for user authorization.
identity.detailedDescription: The Identity API provides OAuth2 authentication flow integration, allowing extensions to authenticate users with Google services and other OAuth2 providers. It handles the complex OAuth2 flow including token refresh and secure storage.

management.name: management
management.shortDescription: Manage other installed apps/extensions. Required for this manager to function.
management.detailedDescription: The Management API allows extensions to query and manage other installed extensions and apps. This includes getting detailed information, enabling/disabling, uninstalling, and monitoring installation events. This is the core permission for extension managers like modcore EM.
mitigationTips: This permission is required for modcore EM to function
Be cautious when enabling/disabling other extensions
Verify actions with users before execution

nativeMessaging.name: nativeMessaging
nativeMessaging.shortDescription: Communicate with native applications on the user's computer.
nativeMessaging.detailedDescription: The NativeMessaging API allows extensions to exchange messages with native applications installed on the user's computer. This bridges the gap between web extensions and native code, enabling access to system resources and capabilities not available to web technologies.
mitigationTips: Only communicate with trusted native hosts
Validate all messages from native apps

notifications.name: notifications
notifications.shortDescription: Create and display rich desktop notifications.
notifications.detailedDescription: The Notifications API allows extensions to display system notifications to the user. These appear outside the browser window and can include icons, images, buttons, and progress indicators. Notifications persist until dismissed or interacted with.
mitigationTips: Respect user's Do Not Disturb settings

proxy.name: proxy
proxy.detailedDescription: The Proxy API allows extensions to control the browser's proxy settings, routing all browser traffic through specified proxy servers. This affects all network traffic from the browser and can be used to bypass restrictions, enhance privacy, or route traffic through specific networks.
mitigationTips: Clearly explain proxy changes to users
Provide easy way to disable proxy

scripting.name: scripting
scripting.shortDescription: Inject scripts/CSS into web pages. Can read/modify page content.
scripting.detailedDescription: The Scripting API allows extensions to inject JavaScript and CSS into web pages. This enables extensions to modify page content, extract data, add functionality, and interact with page elements. Combined with host permissions, this provides extensive access to web page content and behavior.
mitigationTips: Clean up injected content when done

storage.name: storage
storage.detailedDescription: The Storage API provides a persistent storage mechanism for extension data. Unlike localStorage, this is isolated to the extension, has larger quotas, syncs across devices (storage.sync), and is available in service workers. Data persists until explicitly cleared.
mitigationTips: Use appropriate storage area (local vs sync)

tabs.name: tabs
tabs.shortDescription: Access and manipulate browser tabs. Does not grant content access without host permissions.
tabs.detailedDescription: The Tabs API allows extensions to create, modify, reorder, query, and interact with browser tabs. This includes accessing tab URLs, titles, favicons, taking screenshots, and injecting scripts (with host permissions). Without host permissions, it cannot read page content.

webNavigation.name: webNavigation
webNavigation.shortDescription: Receive notifications about navigation request status.
webNavigation.detailedDescription: The WebNavigation API allows extensions to monitor navigation events in browser tabs and frames. This includes events for navigation start, completion, error, and transitions between different document states. This provides detailed insight into page loading processes.
mitigationTips: Be aware this reveals browsing patterns
Clean up event listeners when not needed

webRequest.name: webRequest
webRequest.shortDescription: Observe, analyze, and modify network requests.
webRequest.detailedDescription: The WebRequest API allows extensions to intercept, analyze, block, and modify network requests and responses. This provides visibility into all HTTP traffic and the ability to modify headers, block requests, or redirect to different URLs. Extremely powerful for network-level modifications.
mitigationTips: Handle large numbers of requests efficiently

webRequestBlocking.name: webRequestBlocking
webRequestBlocking.shortDescription: Block or modify network requests synchronously.
webRequestBlocking.detailedDescription: The WebRequestBlocking permission allows extensions to block or modify network requests synchronously, meaning the browser will wait for the extension's response before proceeding. This is used for critical modifications like blocking requests or redirecting URLs. It can significantly impact performance if not used carefully.
mitigationTips: Use declarativeNetRequest when possible in MV3
Minimize blocking time to avoid performance issues
Handle edge cases to prevent breaking sites

webRequestAuthProvider.name: webRequestAuthProvider
webRequestAuthProvider.shortDescription: Provide authentication credentials for network requests.
webRequestAuthProvider.detailedDescription: The WebRequestAuthProvider API allows extensions to respond to authentication challenges by providing credentials. This is used for handling HTTP authentication (Basic, Digest, NTLM) and can be used to implement custom authentication flows or integrate with password managers.
mitigationTips: Only provide credentials for necessary domains
Implement proper error handling for auth failures

activeTab.name: activeTab
activeTab.shortDescription: Grants temporary access to the active tab when the user invokes the extension.
activeTab.detailedDescription: The activeTab permission grants temporary access to the currently active tab only when the user invokes the extension (via click, keyboard shortcut, etc.). This is a privacy-friendly alternative to broad host permissions, as it requires user action and only affects the current tab.
mitigationTips: This is the safest way to access page content
Combine with scripting for content modifications
Clearly indicate when extension is active

background.name: background
background.shortDescription: Allows the extension to run a service worker in the background.
background.detailedDescription: The background permission (specified as 'service_worker' in manifest) allows extensions to run persistent or event-based scripts in the background. In MV3, this is a service worker that handles events, maintains state, and performs background tasks even when no extension pages are open.
mitigationTips: Use offscreen documents for DOM APIs

clipboardRead.name: clipboardRead
clipboardRead.shortDescription: Read data from the system clipboard.
clipboardRead.detailedDescription: The ClipboardRead permission allows extensions to read content from the system clipboard. This requires user interaction (like a click) in most cases and can access text, images, and other clipboard data formats.
mitigationTips: Only read clipboard when user initiates action

clipboardWrite.name: clipboardWrite
clipboardWrite.shortDescription: Write data to the system clipboard.
clipboardWrite.detailedDescription: The ClipboardWrite permission allows extensions to write content to the system clipboard. This enables 'copy to clipboard' functionality and can write text, HTML, and images. Generally safer than clipboardRead but still requires careful handling.
mitigationTips: Support multiple clipboard formats when appropriate

host.name: host
host.shortDescription: Access specific websites or patterns (URLs). Grants ability to read and change data on matching sites.
host.detailedDescription: Host permissions allow extensions to interact with specific websites or URL patterns. When combined with content scripts or certain APIs, this grants the ability to read and modify page content, inject scripts, access cookies, and monitor network requests for those sites. The risk varies significantly based on the specificity of the pattern.
mitigationTips: Use specific patterns instead of broad wildcards
Clearly explain why each site is needed
Consider optional permissions for additional sites

riskVariations.all_urls: Grants access to ALL websites - highest risk
riskVariations.broad_wildcard: Access to many sites via wildcard patterns - high risk
riskVariations.specific_sites: Access to specific named sites - medium risk
riskVariations.single_site: Access to one specific site - lower risk

unlimitedStorage.name: unlimitedStorage
unlimitedStorage.detailedDescription: The UnlimitedStorage permission removes the default 5MB quota limit for chrome.storage.local, allowing extensions to store larger amounts of data. This does not affect chrome.storage.sync which has its own limits. Useful for extensions that need to cache substantial data locally.
mitigationTips: Consider IndexedDB for very large data

sidePanel.name: sidePanel
sidePanel.shortDescription: Control UI in the browser's side panel.
sidePanel.detailedDescription: The SidePanel API allows extensions to create and control content in Chrome's side panel - a persistent sidebar that stays open while browsing. This provides a dedicated space for extension UI alongside the main content area.
mitigationTips: Make panel content useful when open
Consider responsive design for narrow width

offscreen.name: offscreen
offscreen.shortDescription: Create and manage offscreen documents for APIs not available in service workers.
offscreen.detailedDescription: The Offscreen API allows extensions to create hidden, non-interactive documents that can use DOM APIs not available in service workers. This bridges the gap between service worker limitations and the need for certain web platform features.
mitigationTips: Close offscreen documents when not needed
Use only when service worker APIs are insufficient

userScripts.name: userScripts
userScripts.shortDescription: Register user scripts that run in isolated worlds and only on declared host permissions.
userScripts.detailedDescription: The UserScripts API allows extensions to register custom scripts that users can configure to run on specific websites. These run in isolated worlds and only on sites with declared host permissions, providing a safer alternative to traditional content scripts for user customization.
mitigationTips: Provide clear security warnings to users

tabCapture.name: tabCapture
tabCapture.shortDescription: Capture visible tab content as a media stream.
tabCapture.detailedDescription: The TabCapture API allows extensions to capture the content of browser tabs as a MediaStream. This can be used for recording, streaming, or processing tab content in real-time. Requires activeTab or specific host permissions.
mitigationTips: Clearly indicate when capture is active

tabGroups.name: tabGroups
tabGroups.detailedDescription: The TabGroups API allows extensions to create, modify, and manage tab groups. This includes creating groups, adding/removing tabs from groups, changing group colors and titles, and collapsing/expanding groups.

topSites.name: topSites
topSites.shortDescription: Access the list of most visited sites.
topSites.detailedDescription: The TopSites API allows extensions to access the user's most frequently visited websites as determined by the browser. This provides insight into user browsing habits and can be used for quick access features or analytics.
mitigationTips: This reveals browsing patterns - handle carefully
Allow users to disable this feature

sessions.name: sessions
sessions.shortDescription: Query and restore recently closed tabs or windows.
sessions.detailedDescription: The Sessions API allows extensions to access and restore recently closed tabs and windows. This enables 'undo close' functionality and session management features that go beyond the current window state.

readingList.name: readingList
readingList.shortDescription: Read, add, and remove items from the Reading List.
readingList.detailedDescription: The ReadingList API allows extensions to interact with the browser's Reading List feature. Extensions can add pages to the reading list, query existing items, update their state, and remove items.

printerProvider.name: printerProvider
printerProvider.shortDescription: Implement a printer provider, exposing printers to the browser.
printerProvider.detailedDescription: The PrinterProvider API allows extensions to implement custom printer backends, making printers available to the browser that wouldn't otherwise be accessible. This enables cloud printing, network printing, and virtual printer implementations.

printing.name: printing
printing.detailedDescription: The Printing API allows extensions to submit print jobs programmatically. This enables automated printing workflows, custom print dialogs, and integration with specialized printing systems.

privacy.name: privacy
privacy.detailedDescription: The Privacy API allows extensions to control various privacy-related browser settings including network prediction, safe browsing, spell checking, and other privacy-sensitive features. This affects how the browser handles user data and interacts with services.

processes.name: processes
processes.shortDescription: Access information about the browser's processes.
processes.detailedDescription: The Processes API allows extensions to access information about browser processes including memory usage, CPU utilization, and process IDs. This is useful for system monitoring and performance analysis tools.

system.cpu.name: system.cpu
system.cpu.detailedDescription: The System.CPU API allows extensions to query information about the computer's processor including architecture, model name, and feature support. This is read-only metadata useful for system information tools.

system.memory.name: system.memory
system.memory.detailedDescription: The System.Memory API allows extensions to query information about the computer's physical memory (RAM) including total capacity and available memory. This helps extensions adapt their behavior based on system resources.
mitigationTips: Use memory info for optimization decisions
Don't rely on exact memory values

system.storage.name: system.storage
system.storage.detailedDescription: The System.Storage API allows extensions to query information about attached storage devices including capacity, usage, and type. This enables storage management features and system information tools.

system.display.name: system.display
system.display.detailedDescription: The System.Display API allows extensions to query information about connected displays including resolution, DPI, and display arrangement. This enables multi-monitor aware features and display optimization.

tts.name: tts
tts.detailedDescription: The TTS (Text-to-Speech) API allows extensions to use the browser's built-in speech synthesis to read text aloud. This includes voice selection, rate control, pitch adjustment, and event callbacks for speech progress.

ttsEngine.name: ttsEngine
ttsEngine.detailedDescription: The TTSEngine API allows extensions to implement custom text-to-speech voices that can be used by the browser and other extensions. This enables third-party speech synthesis backends and specialized voice implementations.

vpnProvider.name: vpnProvider
vpnProvider.detailedDescription: The VPNProvider API allows extensions to implement VPN (Virtual Private Network) clients that can tunnel all browser traffic through a secure connection. This is a powerful networking feature that affects all browser traffic routing.

certificateProvider.name: certificateProvider
certificateProvider.shortDescription: Provide client certificates to the browser.
certificateProvider.detailedDescription: The CertificateProvider API allows extensions to provide client certificates for mutual TLS authentication. This enables smart card integration, enterprise certificate management, and custom authentication flows.

fileSystemProvider.name: fileSystemProvider
fileSystemProvider.shortDescription: Create virtual file systems for the ChromeOS file manager.
fileSystemProvider.detailedDescription: The FileSystemProvider API allows extensions to create virtual file systems that appear in the ChromeOS Files app. This enables cloud storage integration, network file access, and custom file backends.

platformKeys.name: platformKeys
platformKeys.shortDescription: Access client certificates managed by the OS.
platformKeys.detailedDescription: The PlatformKeys API allows extensions to access client certificates and keys managed by the operating system or hardware security modules. This provides integration with enterprise PKI systems and hardware tokens.

enterprise.platformKeys.name: enterprise.platformKeys
enterprise.platformKeys.shortDescription: Access enterprise client certificates on managed devices.
enterprise.platformKeys.detailedDescription: The Enterprise.PlatformKeys API provides access to enterprise-managed client certificates on ChromeOS and managed devices. This is specifically for enterprise scenarios with administrator-controlled certificates.

hid.name: hid
hid.detailedDescription: The HID API allows extensions to communicate with USB HID devices like keyboards, mice, gamepads, and specialized input devices. This enables custom device support and advanced input handling.

usb.name: usb
usb.detailedDescription: The USB API allows extensions to communicate with USB devices directly. This provides low-level access to USB protocols and enables support for a wide range of hardware peripherals beyond standard HID devices.

serial.name: serial
serial.detailedDescription: The Serial API allows extensions to communicate with devices via serial ports (RS-232, USB-to-Serial, etc.). This enables integration with hardware that uses serial communication protocols.
mitigationTips: Handle baud rate and protocol settings

networking.config.name: networking.config
networking.config.detailedDescription: The Networking.Config API allows extensions to configure network settings including proxy configurations. This affects how the browser connects to the internet and can route traffic through specific servers.

idle.name: idle
idle.shortDescription: Detect when the user's machine idle state changes.
idle.detailedDescription: The Idle API allows extensions to detect when the user's computer enters or exits an idle state based on keyboard and mouse activity. This enables power-saving features and activity-aware behavior.

power.name: power
power.detailedDescription: The Power API allows extensions to prevent the system from sleeping or the display from dimming. This is useful for presentations, long-running tasks, or keeping the screen active during specific activities.
mitigationTips: Only keep system awake when necessary
Clearly indicate power override is active
Provide easy way to release power lock
Respect battery status on mobile devices

gcm.name: gcm
gcm.shortDescription: Receive messages via Google Cloud Messaging.
gcm.detailedDescription: The GCM (Google Cloud Messaging) API allows extensions to receive push messages from servers via Google's messaging infrastructure. This enables real-time updates and server-initiated communication even when the extension isn't actively running.

mdns.name: mdns
mdns.detailedDescription: The mDNS API allows extensions to discover services on the local network using multicast DNS (Bonjour/ZeroConf). This enables discovery of network printers, Chromecast devices, IoT devices, and other local services.

dns.name: dns
dns.detailedDescription: The DNS API allows extensions to perform DNS lookups programmatically. This enables custom DNS resolution, DNS-based service discovery, and network diagnostics.

wallpaper.name: wallpaper
wallpaper.detailedDescription: The Wallpaper API allows extensions to set the desktop wallpaper on ChromeOS devices. This enables custom wallpaper applications, dynamic wallpapers, and wallpaper management tools.

input.ime.name: input.ime
input.ime.shortDescription: Implement a custom Input Method Editor (IME).
input.ime.detailedDescription: The Input.IME API allows extensions to implement custom input methods for text entry. This enables virtual keyboards, handwriting input, speech-to-text input, and specialized input methods for different languages or use cases.

accessibilityFeatures.read.name: accessibilityFeatures.read
accessibilityFeatures.read.detailedDescription: The AccessibilityFeatures.Read API allows extensions to query the state of accessibility features like screen reader support, high contrast mode, and other assistive technology settings. This is read-only access to accessibility configuration.
mitigationTips: Use to adapt UI for accessibility needs

accessibilityFeatures.modify.name: accessibilityFeatures.modify
accessibilityFeatures.modify.shortDescription: Modify accessibility settings such as screen magnifier or high contrast mode.
accessibilityFeatures.modify.detailedDescription: The AccessibilityFeatures.Modify API allows extensions to change accessibility settings including screen magnifier, high contrast mode, and other assistive features. This can significantly alter the user's experience and should be used carefully.

loginState.name: loginState
loginState.shortDescription: Read the user's login state (signed in or not).
loginState.detailedDescription: The LoginState API allows extensions to determine if the user is currently signed into their Google account on ChromeOS. This enables features that depend on authentication state without requiring full identity permissions.
mitigationTips: Handle both logged in and out states

documentScan.name: documentScan
documentScan.detailedDescription: The DocumentScan API allows extensions to access document scanners connected to the system. This enables scanning documents directly from the browser without external applications.

fileBrowserHandler.name: fileBrowserHandler
fileBrowserHandler.detailedDescription: The FileBrowserHandler API allows extensions to add custom actions to the ChromeOS Files app context menu. This enables integration with cloud storage, file processing, and custom file operations.

fontSettings.name: fontSettings
fontSettings.detailedDescription: The FontSettings API allows extensions to query and modify browser font settings including default fonts, minimum font sizes, and encoding settings. This affects how text is rendered across all websites.

pageCapture.name: pageCapture
pageCapture.shortDescription: Save a web page as MHTML format.
pageCapture.detailedDescription: The PageCapture API allows extensions to save complete web pages as MHTML (MIME HTML) archives. This captures the entire page including resources like images and CSS for offline viewing.
mitigationTips: Provide progress feedback for large pages

searchProvider.name: searchProvider
searchProvider.shortDescription: Replace or modify the default search engine.
searchProvider.detailedDescription: The SearchProvider API allows extensions to modify the browser's default search engine settings. This affects omnibox searches, right-click searches, and other search functionality. This is a powerful setting that significantly affects user experience.

storageManaged.name: storageManaged
storageManaged.shortDescription: Access read-only configuration set by enterprise admins.
storageManaged.detailedDescription: The Storage.Managed API provides read-only access to storage areas configured by enterprise administrators via policies. This allows extensions to read organization-specific settings without being able to modify them.

system.network.name: system.network
system.network.detailedDescription: The System.Network API allows extensions to query information about network interfaces including MAC addresses, IP addresses, and connection types. This provides network configuration details for system information and diagnostics.
mitigationTips: Don't transmit network details externally without consent

webAuthenticationProxy.name: webAuthenticationProxy
webAuthenticationProxy.detailedDescription: The WebAuthenticationProxy API allows extensions to intercept and handle WebAuthn (Web Authentication) requests. This enables hardware security key integration, custom authenticators, and enterprise authentication flows.

printingMetrics.name: printingMetrics
printingMetrics.detailedDescription: The PrintingMetrics API allows extensions to query statistics about printer usage including print job counts, pages printed, and printer status. This enables print monitoring and accounting features.

runtime.name: runtime
runtime.shortDescription: Basic runtime info, event listeners, and message passing.
runtime.detailedDescription: The Runtime API provides core extension functionality including lifecycle events, message passing between extension components, platform information, and update handling. This is automatically available to all extensions and doesn't need to be declared.

search.name: search
search.detailedDescription: The Search API allows extensions to perform searches using the browser's default search engine. This provides a way to execute searches and get results without needing to know which search engine is configured.

windows.name: windows
windows.detailedDescription: The Windows API allows extensions to create, modify, and manage browser windows. This includes window positioning, sizing, focus management, and state control (minimized, maximized, fullscreen).

devtools.name: devtools
devtools.detailedDescription: The DevTools API allows extensions to create panels, sidebars, and custom inspectors within Chrome DevTools. This enables development tools, debugging aids, and performance analyzers that integrate directly into the developer workflow.
mitigationTips: Don't expose sensitive information in DevTools panels

privacy.name: Privacy
privacy.description: Permissions that affect user privacy and data handling

security.name: Security
security.description: Permissions related to browser and system security

functionality.name: Functionality
functionality.description: Permissions that enable core extension features

accessibility.name: Accessibility
accessibility.description: Permissions for assistive technologies and accessibility features

data.name: Data Access
data.description: Permissions that provide access to user data

medium.name: Medium Risk
medium.description: May access sensitive data or browser functions

high.name: High Risk
high.description: Grants extensive access to data and browser behavior

varies.name: Varies
varies.description: Risk depends on the specific implementation
